rationale.D1: All subsystems (Shooter, Feeder, Intake, Angler, Windexer) own TalonFX hardware directly with no IO interface layer; the CTRE swerve generator provides a vendored drivetrain abstraction, which earns at most L2, but mechanisms are entirely hardware-welded.
rationale.D2: Every mechanism carries a current/requested state enum pair with a requestState() API and periodic switch dispatch (confirmed in Shooter.java, AutoStateMachine.java, TargetTracking.java), which is the 2910-style explicit per-subsystem FSM; there is no single cross-robot coordinator fanning out goals, so this is L2, not L3.
rationale.D3: Robot.java extends LoggedRobot and Drivetrain.startSimThread() wires CTRE's updateSimState, but simulationPeriodic() is empty and no WPILib physics classes (ElevatorSim, FlywheelSim, etc.) exist; this is a token sim stub, not mechanism physics.
rationale.D4: No src/test directory and no test files exist; no CI workflow found in .github; confirmed zero test infrastructure.
rationale.D5: Robot.java is a LoggedRobot with WPILOGWriter to /home/lvuser/logs and NT4Publisher confirmed running, and Telemetry.java uses Logger.recordOutput for drive module telemetry, but no @AutoLog inputs structs or processInputs calls exist anywhere — coverage is drivetrain-only, so this is L2 (structured logging, not full AdvantageKit).
rationale.D6: 26 PathPlanner .path files confirmed on disk and AutoStateMachine.java constructs PathPlannerPath arrays and calls AutoBuilder.followPath() in competition autos; no Choreo files present; confirmed L2 PathPlanner adoption with multiple named competition autos.
rationale.D7: Vision.java uses PhotonCamera + PhotonPoseEstimator (MULTI_TAG_PNP_ON_COPROCESSOR, two cameras) and PoseEstimation.java calls m_drivetrain.addVisionMeasurement() with timestamps for both cameras; standard devs are set (1.5,1.5,1.5) but there is no dynamic std-dev tuning or rejection logic beyond an ambiguity check, placing this at L2.
rationale.D8: WarlordsLib is a tracked separate library repo confirmed on disk alongside 5 seasons of tracked repos (2022-2026); no README in the 2026 season repo, no CI workflows, no formatter configuration, so the library presence earns L2 but CI and docs are absent.
